title: Versant Media Spin Off And Free TV Deal Reshape Investor Focus
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Versant Media Group (NasdaqGS:VSNT) completed its spin off from Comcast in January 2026, becoming a fully independent media company. The company acquired Free TV Networks to enter free over the air digital broadcast and ad supported streaming TV channels.

Versant Media Group, now trading independently under NasdaqGS:VSNT, last closed at $33.59. The share price is up 3.5% over the past week, while the 30 day and year to date returns show declines of 27.8% and 28.0%. This context illustrates how the market has recently been reacting to the stock.

As an independent company with a new foothold in free broadcast and ad supported streaming, Versant Media Group is repositioning itself within the media sector. The spin off and acquisition give investors fresh events to watch as the company works to build its own identity and audience reach outside Comcast.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Versant Media Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Versant Media Group.NasdaqGS:VSNT 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Why Versant Media Group could be great value

The spin off from Comcast and the purchase of Free TV Networks together signal that Versant Media Group is trying to reposition itself in the TV and streaming value chain, closer to ad supported and free to access viewers. For investors, that puts Versant in more direct comparison with media groups that blend traditional TV with streaming, such as Paramount Global, Fox Corporation and Disney, where advertising, carriage fees and digital revenue often pull in different directions.

How this fits into the Versant Media Group narrative

Coverage from Wolfe Research with an Outperform rating and Goldman Sachs with a Neutral stance suggests analysts are still forming their views on how Versant’s mix of basic cable networks, digital platforms and now free over the air channels could play out. The recent share price weakness alongside ongoing analyst interest may indicate that professional investors are watching execution on the new strategy rather than treating the spin off and acquisition as a finished story.

Risks and rewards to keep in mind

Expansion into free over the air and ad supported streaming gives Versant more ways to reach audiences and advertisers across different price points. Coverage from multiple brokerages, including an Outperform recommendation in the mix, shows the company is firmly on the radar of institutional investors. Integrating Free TV Networks into Versant’s existing portfolio could be operationally complex and may take time before the benefits are clear in reported results. Competition for advertising budgets from larger media groups and pure play streamers could limit the upside from the new channels if Versant struggles to differentiate its offering.

Story Continues

What to watch next

From here, investors may want to watch how quickly Versant embeds Free TV Networks into its distribution, advertising sales and content pipeline, and whether analyst estimates or ratings shift as more post spin off data becomes available. If you want to see how other investors are framing the story and what they are focused on, check community narratives on Versant Media Group’s dedicated page.

title: Goldman boosts TSMC price target, Comcast spinoff to begin trading
content:
Morning Brief host Julie Hyman tracks several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) shares ticking higher in premarket trading after Goldman Sachs analysts raise their price target, Mobileye (MBLY) announces a deal with an unnamed US automaker, and Comcast (CMCSA) spinoff Versant Media Group (VSNT, VSNTV) begins its trading on the Nasdaq Monday.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

Video Transcript

00:00 Speaker A

First up, Taiwan Semi. That stock is rising on the heels of an upbeat analyst note from Goldman Sachs, which raised its price target on the stock by 35%. The chipmaker riding the wave of the AI boom and optimism that demand will continue to surge in 2026. Goldman analysts say in the note that AI will be a quote multi-year growth engine for TSMC.

00:20 Speaker A

Next is Mobile Eye. That stock is jumping ahead of the opening bell on Wall Street after the company said it secured a deal with a major US automaker for its chips, but it didn't say which one.

00:29 Speaker A

As a result, Mobileye lifted its production outlook and the automaker will include the company's self-driving technology in millions of vehicles. It comes after a tough year for the company. Shares were nearly cut in half in 2025.

00:40 Speaker A

And finally, Comcast. The telecom erased earlier losses as the company starts a new era without its portfolio of cable TV channels. The company's completed its spin-off of those businesses into Versent, which starts trading today on the Nasdaq under the Tible VSNT. Shares of Versent also pointing to declines at the open but on low volume. Comcast shareholders got one share of Versent for every 25 shares of Comcast they held as of December 16th.

title: Versant Media to Begin Trading After Comcast Spinoff
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Ahead of its January 5 market debut, Versant Media Group (VSNT) is preparing to trade as an independent, publicly listed media company following a tax-free, pro rata spinoff from Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA). The separation gives investors direct exposure to a focused portfolio of NBCUniversal's traditional cable networks and related digital brands that were previously housed within Comcast's broader conglomerate. The new entity brings together linear cable assets such as CNBC, USA Network, E!, SYFY, Oxygen, and Golf Channel with a set of digital properties including Fandango, Rotten Tomatoes, Peacock-adjacent Now-branded news offerings, GolfPass and GolfNow, and youth and sports platform SportsEngine, with management indicating the balance sheet is expected to be conservative at launch.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 7 Warning Signs with ECVT. Is VSNTV fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Speaking at an investor day ahead of the transaction, CEO Mark Lazarus described Versant as a company built to extend beyond cable and potentially beyond traditional media, framing the spinoff as an opportunity to innovate across both linear and digital channels. Lazarus noted that the company's networks accounted for 14 billion hours of viewed content in 2024, with approximately 65 million households watching monthly, while its digital platforms process around 140 million transactions each year, including movie ticketing and golf tee-time reservations. The move also comes as Comcast continues to streamline its strategic focus, following reports that a previously discussed bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) involving NBCUniversal assets is no longer active, as Warner Bros. instead explores a potential transaction with Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) and faces a hostile approach from Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY).

From an investor perspective, the spinoff could simplify Comcast's equity narrative by allowing its remaining business to trade more directly on connectivity, theme parks, and premium streaming, while Versant may gain flexibility around M&A, cost actions, and capital returns as a standalone company. Some market commentators have suggested that despite skepticism around legacy cable exposure, an expected valuation of roughly $10 billion could imply a double-digit free cash flow yield and a 6x to 7x EV/EBITDA multiple, potentially below peers such as Fox and Paramount Skydance. Under the transaction terms, Comcast shareholders will receive one Versant share for every 25 Comcast shares held on the record date, a structure that could have knock-on implications across the media sector, including companies such as AMC Networks, Starz Entertainment, Fox Corporation, Nexstar Media Group, E.W. Scripps Company, and Gray Television, as well as ETFs with significant Comcast exposure.

title: What's next for Versant as networks spin off from Comcast
content:
[CNBC Peacock News Logo on Storefront]
Lokibaho

Versant Media Group (VSNT) will start trading on January 5 after the newly formed, independent media company is separated from Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) through a tax-free pro rata spinoff. The media company will house most of NBCUniversal’s traditional cable television networks and associated digital brands, giving public investors direct exposure to a focused portfolio of linear and digital media assets that had previously been embedded within Comcast's (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) broader conglomerate structure.

The hybrid cable-digital media company will be anchored by well-known cable channels such as CNBC, USA Network, E!, SYFY, Oxygen, Golf Channel, and other NBCUniversal cable networks, along with a digital portfolio that includes Fandango, Rotten Tomatoes, Peacock-adjacent brands like Now-branded news offerings, GolfPass/GolfNow, and youth/sports software platform SportsEngine. Ahead of the spinoff, Versant (VSNT) management emphasized that the entity will be well capitalized and conservatively leveraged at launch.

"Versant (VSNT) has a unique set of assets, a dedicated leadership team, and we are prepared to defy expectations -- be innovators in the markets that we serve," CEO Mark Lazarus said at the investor day (see the full presentation [https://www.cmcsa.com/static-files/d25d54ef-5b4d-4fea-9b11-8ceec547e2b4]). "This is a company with a mandate to build beyond cable, in fact, beyond media, and we are ready to transform and positioning ourselves to win," he added.

The move puts Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) firmly in the vanguard of companies looking to separate their slower-growing legacy pay TV networks from smaller (but faster-growing) streaming operations. Those networks accounted for 14 billion hours of viewed content in 2024, Lazarus noted, with 65 million households watching its networks monthly. In addition, its digital properties (including reservation systems for golf tee times and movie tickets via its Fandango/Rotten Tomatoes brands) process 140 million transactions per year. Even as plans for the spin-off were proceeding, Comcast entertained a bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]) that would involve merging its NBCUniversal division with WBD's studio operations and theme parks. That bid appears to be off, with Warner Bros. aiming for a transaction with Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) and facing a hostile buyout from Paramount Skydance (PSKY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSKY]).

By separating Versant (VSNT), Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) sees its equity story being simpler for investors to understand, allowing the stock to trade more directly on growth in connectivity, parks, and premium streaming. For its part, Versant (VSNT) may find it easier to consider M&A and be more flexible with cost restructuring and capital returns.

Seeking Alpha analyst WideAlpha noted that even though many skeptics may view Versant (VSNT) as a "melting ice cube", the expected valuation of roughly $10 billion would mean a double-digit free cash flow yield and a 6X to 7X EV/EBITDA multiple. "This would be below some peers facing similar challenges, such as Fox and Paramount Skydance. Versant is also expected to start its public life with a healthy balance sheet, and as a separate entity, it is more likely to get takeover interest as consolidation continues in the industry," read part of the breakdown from WideAlpha. It's an undemanding valuation for some of the industry's top brands, including CNBC, they noted. Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) may deserve its premium as the streaming leader, but it's harder to argue that Fox (FOX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FOX]) (FOXA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FOXA]) or Paramount Skydance (PSKY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSKY]) deserve a significant premium over Versant (VSNT).

The spinoff transaction is structured as a pro rata distribution of one Versant (VSNT) share for every 25 Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) shares held by shareholders on the record date. Media companies that the emergence of Versant (VSNT) could have some implications for to varying degrees include Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]), AMC Networks (AMCX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMCX]), Starz Entertainment (STRZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STRZ]), Fox Corporation (FOXA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FOXA]), Paramount Skydance (PSKY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSKY]), Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]), Nexstar Media Group (NXST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NXST]), E.W. Scripps Company (SSP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SSP]), and Gray Television (GTN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GTN]).

ETFs with the heaviest weighting of Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]) are the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Communication Services ETF (RSPC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RSPC]), State Street® Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLC]), and iShares U.S. Telecommunications ETF (IYZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IYZ]).

title: UiPath, Versant Media to join S&P MidCap 400 and SmallCap 600
content:
* S&P Dow Jones Indices said on Tuesday that UiPath (PATH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PATH]) will replace Synovus Financial (SNV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNV]) in the S&P MidCap 400, effective January 2, 2026, following Synovus’s pending acquisition by Pinnacle Financial Partners (PNFP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PNFP]).
 	* Versant Media Group (VSNT) will replace Brandywine Realty Trust (BDN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BDN]) in the S&P SmallCap 600, effective January 6, 2026, after its spin-off from Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]), which will remain in the S&P 500. 

 	* CMCSA -0.0% after hours to $29.38.
 	* Source: Press Release [https://seekingalpha.com/pr/20348668-uipath-set-to-join-s-and-p-midcap-400-and-versant-media-group-to-join-s-and-p-smallcap-600]

title: UiPath Set to Join S&P MidCap 400 and Versant Media Group to Join S&P SmallCap 600
content:
NEW YORK, Dec. 23, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- S&P Dow Jones Indices will make the following changes to the S&P MidCap 400, S&P SmallCap 600:

UiPath Inc. (NYSE: PATH) will replace Synovus Financial Corp. (NYSE: SNV) in the S&P MidCap 400 effective prior to the opening of trading on Friday, January 2. S&P MidCap 400 constituent Pinnacle Financial Partners Inc. (NASD: PNFP) is acquiring Synovus Financial Corp in a deal expected to be completed soon, pending final closing conditions. Versant Media Group Inc. (NASD: VSNT) will replace Brandywine Realty Trust (NYSE: BDN) in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective prior to the opening of trading on Tuesday, January 6. S&P 500 constituent Comcast Corp. (NASD: CMCSA) is spinning off Versant Media Group in a transaction expected to be completed January 5. Comcast will remain in the S&P 500 following completion of the spin-off.

Following is a summary of the changes that will take place prior to the open of trading on the effective date:

Effective Date Index Name  Action Company Name Ticker GICS Sector January 2, 2026 S&P MidCap 400 Addition UiPath PATH Information Technology January 2, 2026 S&P MidCap 400 Deletion Synovus Financial SNV Financials January 6, 2026 S&P SmallCap 600 Addition Versant Media Group VSNT Communication Services January 6, 2026 S&P SmallCap 600 Deletion Brandywine Realty Trust BDN Real Estate

ABOUT S&P DOW JONES INDICES

S&P Dow Jones Indices is the largest global resource for essential index-based concepts, data and research, and home to iconic financial market indicators, such as the S&P 500® and the Dow Jones Industrial Average®. More assets are invested in products based on our indices than products based on indices from any other provider in the world. Since Charles Dow invented the first index in 1884, S&P DJI has been innovating and developing indices across the spectrum of asset classes helping to define the way investors measure and trade the markets.

S&P Dow Jones Indices is a division of S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI), which provides essential intelligence for individuals, companies, and governments to make decisions with confidence. For more information, visit www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/.
